AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT CANNOT FIND GOOD CAUSE TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE AND DOES NOT FIND REPRESENTATION OF DEFENDANT TO BE CREDIBLE. THE COURT DENIED DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL. THE TRIAL DATE OF 10/22/12 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206 IS MAINTAINED. THE COURT FURTHER ORDERED THAT DEFENDANT CAN COME BACK TO GET AN ATTORNEY AND PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE MOTION TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE WHICH IS ALLOWED ON A SHORTENED TIME AND WILL CONSIDER THE MERITS OF THE MOTION AT THAT TIME. DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT LUKE BRUGNARA'S MOTION TO WITHDRAW ADMITTED REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS AND ALLOW DEFENDANT TO SERVE DISCOVERY RESPONSES - THE MOTION TO WITHDRAW REQUESTS DEEMED ADMITTED IS GRANTED. THE COURT'S ORDER OF MARCH 20, 2012 SPECIFICALLY PERMITTED DEFENDANT TO BRING THIS MOTION. MR. BRUGNARA'S INCARCERATION IN SOLITARY CONFINEMENT AND THE FAILURE OF FEDERAL AUTHORITIES TO PROVIDE ACCESS TO HIS MAIL OR TO PHONE CONTACT WITH HIS COUNSEL CONSTITUTES EXCUSABLE NEGLECT SUFFICIENT TO PERMIT THE GRANTING OF RELIEF. ANY CLAIMED PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFFS IS OUTWEIGHED BY THE INTEREST IN HAVING CLAIMS ADJUDICATED ON THEIR MERITS. (NEW ALBERTSON'S, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (2009) 168 CAL.APP.4TH 1403, 1420.) PLAINTIFFS MAY PURSUE ADDITIONAL DISCOVERY RELATED TO THE MATTERS INVOLVED IN THE WITHDRAWN ADMISSIONS, AND DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO BEAR THE COST OF SUCH ADDITIONAL DISCOVERY IN AN AMOUNT TO BE DETERMINED BY THE COURT UPON SUBSEQUENT MOTION BY PLAINTIFFS AFTER THE CONCLUSION OF SUCH DISCOVERY. (CODE CIV. PROC., 2033.300, SUBD. (C).) COURT TO PREPARE ORDER. PRO TEM JUDGE: JIM WEIXEL, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

Motion To Withdraw Admitted Requests For Admissions And Allow Deft. To Serve Discovery Responses And Supporting Memorandum Of P & A
Off Calendar Dec-21-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. FREDERICK KANE, APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.


RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT LUKE BRUGNARA'S Motion To Withdraw Admitted Requests For Admissions And Allow Defendant To Serve Discovery Responses - The motion to withdraw requests deemed admitted is granted. The Court's order of March 20, 2012 specifically permitted Defendant to bring this motion. Mr. Brugnara's incarceration in solitary confinement and the failure of federal authorities to provide access to his mail or to phone contact with his counsel constitutes excusable neglect sufficient to permit the granting of relief. Any claimed prejudice to Plaintiffs is outweighed by the interest in having claims adjudicated on their merits. (New Albertson's, Inc. v. Superior Court (2009) 168 Cal.App.4th 1403, 1420.) Plaintiffs may pursue additional discovery related to the matters involved in the withdrawn admissions, and Defendant is ordered to bear the cost of such additional discovery in an amount to be determined by the Court upon subsequent motion by Plaintiffs after the conclusion of such discovery. (Code Civ. Proc., 2033.300, subd. (c).) Court to prepare order. Pro Tem Judge: Jim Weixel, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/jpt

Plaintiffs Al Ebneter, Ebneter Investment Corporation And Ebneter Corp. Money Purchase Plan'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Request For Admissions Admitted, To Compel Responses To Discovery, And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-20-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No Appearances - Court adopted its tentative ruling, Plaintiffs Al Ebneter, Ebneter Investment Corporation And Ebneter Corp. Money Purchase Plan'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Request For Admissions Admitted, To Compel Responses To Discovery, And For Monetary Sanctions. Granted in part. Defendant Luke Brugnara shall provide responses to requests for Admissions, Request for Production of Documents, Special Interrogatories and Form Interrogatories within 120 days of this order. Mr. Brugnara is incarcerated and has substantial justification for not timely responding to the discovery. The requests for admissions are deemed admitted without prejudice to Mr. Burgnara bringing a motion to withdraw the or amend the deemed admissions. Sanctions are denied. Prevailing party prepare court order. Arlene Barton Judge Pro Tem, not reported. (302/JPT)
